---
title: "GDD_Analysis_Fdk"
output: html_document
---

```{R}
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(stringr)
library(broom)
library(tibble)
library(arrow)
```


## Objective
Extend the GDD analysis to all stations in the Fluxdatakit data set that have at least 15 years of recordings. Increasing the number of stations should allow to see regional patterns in GDD at last frost.

Read in parquet file
```{R}
df_all_stations <- read_parquet("../data/data_fdk_parquet/fdk_forest_15years.parquet")
```

Igbp-codes per site
```{R}
df_sites_igbp <- df_all_stations |> 
  distinct(site_id, igbp_land_use)
```


## Analyze yearly trajectory of GDD

Calculate GDD
```{R}
source("../R/GDD.R")
df_GDD <- calculate_gdd(df_all_stations)
```

Plot the mean GPP over time series
```{R}
# Filter out 29th of February, so that every year has 365 days
df_gdd_no_leap <- df_GDD %>%
  mutate(
    year = year(TIMESTAMP),
    month = month(TIMESTAMP),
    day = day(TIMESTAMP)
  ) %>%
  filter(!(month == 2 & day == 29)) %>%
  group_by(site_id, year) %>%
  arrange(TIMESTAMP, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(DOY365 = row_number()) %>%
  ungroup()
df_gdd_mean <- df_gdd_no_leap %>%
  group_by(site_id, DOY365) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_GDD = mean(GDD, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
## Plot mean seasonal trajectory
ggplot(df_gdd_mean, aes(x = DOY365, y = mean_GDD)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ site_id, ncol = 4, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05,0.1)))+
  labs(
    x = "Day of year",
    y = "Mean GDD",
    title = "Mean seasonal GDD trajectory by site"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```


## Analyze GDD at last frost
**Calculate GDD at last frost**
```{R}
source("../R/GDD_LSF.R")
result <- calculate_gdd_lsf(df_all_stations)
df_spring_daily <- result$spring_daily
df_yearly_summary <- result$yearly_summary
# add land use information to yearly summary
site_landuse <- df_all_stations |>
  filter(!is.na(igbp_land_use)) |>
  distinct(site_id, igbp_land_use)

df_yearly_summary <- df_yearly_summary |>
  left_join(site_landuse, by = "site_id")
```

**Plot 5 year average block over time series of each station**
```{R}
# Define 5-year blocks
start_year <- min(df_spring_daily$year, na.rm = TRUE)

df_gdd_blocks <- df_spring_daily |>
  mutate(
    block_start = start_year + 5 * ((year - start_year) %/% 5),
    block_end   = block_start + 4,
    block_label = paste0(block_start, "-", block_end)
  )

# only keep complete 5-year blocks
df_block_counts <- df_gdd_blocks |>
  distinct(site_id, year, block_label, block_start) |>
  count(site_id, block_label, block_start, name = "n_years")

df_gdd_blocks <- df_gdd_blocks |>
  left_join(df_block_counts, by = c("site_id", "block_label", "block_start")) %>%
  filter(n_years == 5)

# Mean per station, 5-year-block and DOY
df_gdd_5yr_mean <- df_gdd_blocks |>
  group_by(site_id, block_label, block_start, DOY) |>
  summarise(
    mean_GDD = mean(GDD, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(block_start)

# Faktor-Level chronologisch setzen
block_levels <- df_gdd_5yr_mean |>
  distinct(block_label, block_start) |>
  arrange(block_start) |>
  pull(block_label)

df_gdd_5yr_mean <- df_gdd_5yr_mean |>
  mutate(block_label = factor(block_label, levels = block_levels))

# Define color scheme
plot_colors <- c(
  "#0072B2", # blue
  "#D55E00", # vermillion
  "#009E73", # bluish green
  "#CC79A7", # reddish purple
  "#E69F00", # orange
  "#56B4E9", # sky blue
  "#F0E442", # yellow
  "#000000"  # black
)

# create plot for each site
dir.create("../figures/5yearGDD", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

sites <- unique(df_gdd_5yr_mean$site_id)

for (s in sites) {
  
  df_plot <- df_gdd_5yr_mean |>
    filter(site_id == s)
  
  if (nrow(df_plot) == 0) next
  
  n_blocks <- dplyr::n_distinct(df_plot$block_label)
  
  p <- ggplot(
    df_plot,
    aes(x = DOY, y = mean_GDD, color = block_label, group = block_label)
  ) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.1))) +
    scale_color_manual(values = plot_colors[seq_len(n_blocks)]) +
    labs(
      x = "Day of year",
      y = "Mean GDD",
      color = "5-year block",
      title = paste("5-year mean spring GDD trajectories -", s)
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
  
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("../figures/5yearGDD/", s, "_5year_mean_GDD.png"),
    plot = p,
    width = 7,
    height = 5,
    dpi = 300
  )
}
```

Compairing 5 year means across stations, it becomes evident, that not all sites show the same trend in the development of 5 year means. For some sites GDD the 5 year means are clearly elevated for the last and second last 5 year period when looking at the total trajectory over the first 150 days but also when focusing on the time window around DOY 100, where the last frost typically occurs, especially at central European sites. The sites that show elevated 5 year means are often located at evergreen needle leaf forests. The most exceptional increase in 5 year mean GDD was observed at FI-Hyy where the 5 year mean from 2016-2020 showed significantly earlier rise in GDD and also unprecedented GDD accumulation in the first 150 days of the year. For other analysed sites the 5 year mean GDD do not show a clear trend of increase especially at US sites. Some of the US sites even show pattern of decreasing 5 year mean GDD around DOY 100 for example US-Bar. 

Boxplot GDD per site:
```{R}
site_groups <- tibble(
  site_id = unique(df_yearly_summary$site_id),
  group = rep(1:ceiling(length(unique(df_yearly_summary$site_id)) / 4), each = 4)[1:length(unique(df_yearly_summary$site_id))]
)

df_plot <- df_yearly_summary |>
  filter(!is.na(GDD_at_LSF), !is.nan(GDD_at_LSF), !is.infinite(GDD_at_LSF)) |>
  left_join(site_groups, by = "site_id")

# Plot with free scales
ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = site_id, y = GDD_at_LSF)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  facet_wrap(~group, scales = "free") +
  labs(
    x = "Site",
    y = "GDD at LSF",
    title = "Distribution of GDD at LSF by site"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

# Plot with fixed y-scale
ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = site_id, y = GDD_at_LSF)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  facet_wrap(~group, scales = "free_x") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 300)) +
  labs(
    x = "Site",
    y = "GDD at LSF",
    title = "Distribution of GDD at LSF by site"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
```

Note: IL-Yat only has one black line, because only once frost. 

Calulate IQR, min, max and range of GDD at LSF
```{R}
df_variability <- df_yearly_summary |>
  group_by(site_id) |>
  summarise(
    n = sum(!is.na(GDD_at_LSF)),
    median_GDD = median(GDD_at_LSF, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR_GDD = IQR(GDD_at_LSF, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_GDD = ifelse(n > 0, min(GDD_at_LSF, na.rm = TRUE), NA),
    max_GDD = ifelse(n > 0, max(GDD_at_LSF, na.rm = TRUE), NA),
    range_GDD = max_GDD - min_GDD
  ) |>
  mutate(
    across(
      c(median_GDD, IQR_GDD, min_GDD, max_GDD, range_GDD),
      \(x) round(x, 1)
    )
  ) |>
  arrange(desc(IQR_GDD))
``` 

The box plots in combination with the statistical measures show significant differences in accumulated GDD at LSF over the site years. The largest IQR occurred at CA-TP1 with 110.4 GDD difference where as DK-Sor showed the smallest IQR with 14.3 GDD difference. Larger IQR also meant larger range (max-min) and higher median GDD at LSF. A number of sites had a min GDD at LSF of 0. More generally high IQR was observed in north-central US. Small IQR was observed at north-eastern US and northern europe. 

Small IQR: US-Bar north eastcoast, US-GLE north central(in the mountains, ENF), US-Ha1 north eastcoast (DBF), US-Ho2 north eastcoast (ENF), DK-Sor northern europe (DBF, on island near sea), FI-Hyy northern europe (ENF, not to far from fjord) --> generally in the north and close to sea so oceanic climate
Large IQR: US-Moz north central (DBF), US-MMS north central (DBF), US-PFa north central (MF), CA-TP1 north central US (ENF) --> continental climate


Plot GDD at last frost for each site over the analysis period 1991-2020:
```{R}
dir.create("../figures/gdd_fdk", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

sites <- unique(df_yearly_summary$site_id)

for (s in sites) {
  
  df_plot <- df_yearly_summary |>
    filter(
      site_id == s,
      !is.na(year),
      !is.na(GDD_at_LSF)
    )
  
  if (nrow(df_plot) < 3) next
  
  mod <- lm(GDD_at_LSF ~ year, data = df_plot)
  
  slope <- coef(mod)[["year"]]
  p_value <- summary(mod)$coefficients["year", "Pr(>|t|)"]
  
  label_text <- paste0(
    "slope = ", round(slope, 2),
    "\n",
    "p = ", signif(p_value, 2)
  )
  
  p <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = year, y = GDD_at_LSF)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#0072B2", linewidth = 0.9) +
    geom_text(
      aes(x = -Inf, y = Inf),
      label = label_text,
      hjust = -0.1,
      vjust = 1.1,
      size = 4,
      inherit.aes = FALSE
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      breaks = seq(1990, 2020, by = 5),
      limits = c(1990, 2020)
    ) +
    labs(
      x = "Year",
      y = "GDD at LSF",
      title = paste("Interannual variation in GDD at the last spring frost -", s)
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
  
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("../figures/gdd_fdk/", s, "_gdd_lsf.png"),
    plot = p,
    width = 7,
    height = 5,
    dpi = 300
  )
}
```

Canada sometimes show negative trends, but they are mostly not statistically significant. 
Switzerland all show positive trends, but not all are statistically significant. 
Germany show no trend. 
Denmark negative trend but not quite statistically significant. 
Spain no significant trend. 
Finland no significant trend.
France has sites with positive and negative trends, not significant.
Italy has positive trends, but mostly not significant. 
Netherlands no trend.
Sweden negative trend, almost statistically significant. 
USA has sites with positive and negative trends. One site with statistically significant positive trend and one site with statistically significant negative trend. More sites show positive trends, then negative trends. 

Across all sites only two sites show statistically significant trends (US-Ha1 increasing; RU-Fyo decreasing). 
For the european sites that show positive trends in GDD at LSF they are often (4/6) ENF and CH-Lae is MF, which means it can potentially also contain a lot of evergreen needle leaf trees. 
At the US sites there is no pattern between igbp land use and positive trends in GDD. 
There does not seem to be a link between the geographical location and the climate zone and positive in GDD at LSF.
For the sites that show negative trend in GDD at LSF they are often (5/6) ENF and 4/6 are in Dfb koppen climate zone. 

In general ENF sites seem to show the most changes in trend of GDD at LSF, but both increasing or decreasing. ---> aufpassen, weil mit Abstand die meisten sites sind ENF

Calculation of z-Score
```{R}
df_anomalies <- df_yearly_summary %>%
  group_by(site_id) %>%
  mutate(
    GDD_at_LSF_mean = mean(GDD_at_LSF, na.rm = TRUE),
    GDD_at_LSF_sd   = sd(GDD_at_LSF, na.rm = TRUE),
    GDD_at_LSF_z    = (GDD_at_LSF - GDD_at_LSF_mean) / GDD_at_LSF_sd,
    
  ) %>%
  ungroup()
```

Plot z-Score
```{R}
dir.create("../figures/gdd_anomalies_fdk", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

df_anomalies <- df_anomalies |>
  mutate(
    highlight = case_when(
      year == 2010 ~ "Event year US",
      year == 2017 ~ "Event year EU",
      TRUE ~ "Other years"
    )
  )

sites <- unique(df_anomalies$site_id)

for (s in sites) {
  
  df_plot <- df_anomalies |>
    filter(
      site_id == s,
      !is.na(year),
      !is.na(GDD_at_LSF_z)
    )
  
  if (nrow(df_plot) == 0) next
  
  p <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = year, y = GDD_at_LSF_z)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = c(-1, 1), linetype = "dotted") +
    geom_point(aes(color = highlight), size = 2) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c(
        "Other years" = "grey60",
        "Event year US" = "#D55E00",
        "Event year EU" = "blue"
      )
    ) +
    labs(
      x = "Year",
      y = "z-score of GDD at LSF",
      color = "",
      title = paste("Standardized anomalies of GDD at the last spring frost -", s)
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )
  
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("../figures/gdd_anomalies_fdk/", s, "_gdd_lsf_z.png"),
    plot = p,
    width = 7,
    height = 5,
    dpi = 300
  )
}
```

A number of European sites show exceptional z-scores in 2017 which corresponds with a known late spring frost event in that year. Only one US site shows an exceptional z-score in 2010. So the known late spring frost event in North-eastern US is not visible in this data.
